sections.paragraph:
m. for מַעֲרֶה (from the root עָרָה)-

(1) nakedness, pudenda, i.q. עֶרְוָה Nah 3:5.

(2) a naked space, i.e. void space. 1Ki 7:36, כְּמַעַד אִישׁ “for the space of each one” (of the borders).

I. מַעֲרָב m. (from the root עָרַב No. I. 2) articles of merchandize, which are interchanged, bartered. Eze 27:9, 27 Eze 27:27, עֹרְבֵי מַעֲבָרֵךְ “those who exchange thy merchandize;” Eze 27:13, 17 Eze 27:17, 19 Eze 27:19, 27 Eze 27:27(beginning), 33 Eze 27:33, 34 Eze 27:34. [In some of its occurrences it appears to mean “a fair or market.” Thes.]

II. מַעֲרָב m. (from the root עָרַב No. II), the West, the part where the sun sets, Psa 75:7 103:12 107:3 Isa 43:5. [Found also with ה local, westward, 1Ch 26:30 and with prefix ל on the west, 2Ch 32:30.]
